assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response clearly defines available as 'a version that's currently supported and can be manually selected' and 'You can upgrade to this version'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response defines default as 'the version that new clusters will get if you don't specify a version' and explains they can differ, noting 'The fact that default (1.31.8) is newer than auto-upgrade target (1.31.7) suggests 1.31.8 was recently promoted and the auto-upgrade system hasn't caught up yet'

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response defines auto-upgrade target as 'the version your cluster will automatically upgrade to during its next maintenance window' but does not explicitly mention it being cluster-specific based on policies

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response explains they can differ and provides reasoning: 'Default advances fastest' and 'Auto-upgrade target is more conservative' with specific example of why 1.31.8 default differs from 1.31.7 auto-upgrade target

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows briefly but does not explain cluster constraints, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or 'no minor' exclusions at all

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response does not recommend any specific gcloud commands or mention get-upgrade-info

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response does not reference GKE release schedule or release notes
